subject: Cloud Computing
title: Rapid Elasticity
description: A micro-lesson that makes Rapid Elasticity usable.

hook.text: A Super Bowl ad airs and traffic spikes 1,000%. Your servers must scale in seconds, not weeks.

buildup.text: Elasticity means resources stretch and shrink automatically based on real-time demand.

discovery.text: Auto-scaling watches your traffic and adds or removes servers without human involvement.

twist.text: Scaling up is easy. Scaling down is the hard part — many systems forget to release resources.

climax.text: Elasticity is why cloud beats owning hardware. You only pay for the capacity you actually use.

punchline.text: The cloud stretches when you need it and shrinks when you don't.

quiz.question: What does 'rapid elasticity' mean in cloud computing?
options: Resources automatically scale up or down based on demand
Cloud providers deliver hardware faster than traditional vendors
Data transfers happen at elastic, variable speeds
